series.product_description: 3M Acrylic Foam Tape GT7100 Series offers very good adhesion performance and high flexibility. These tapes are specifically designed for the attachment of automotive exterior and interior trim applications. Product construction is acrylic pressure sensitive adhesive / acrylic foam / acrylic pressure sensitive adhesive with a translucent red polyethylene liner.
product_features: Provides final adhesion and holding power to automotive paints and substrates.
Meets a number of OEM specifications.
Follows shrinkage and elongation of plastic parts caused by temperature change and has good stress relaxation properties.
Viscoelastic core allows conformity to complex geometries and delivers excellent wet out.
Demonstrates excellent adhesive performance at various temperature conditions.
Offers solvent resistance and high temperature resistance.
main_applications: Attachment of automotive exterior trim applications.
Attachment of automotive interior trim applications.
series.notes: Series-level TDS dated December 2025, supersedes April 2025 version. Typical results are reference-only and not guaranteed specification limits.

params.review_note: Dictionary name is generic peel strength; source values are substrate- and condition-specific typical performance, so use with condition fields and review before final import.
Alias suggests static_shear_70c, but source table is Shear Strength in MPa across multiple conditions, not explicit holding time at 70C. Do not map directly without review.

spec_values.value: Three years from date of manufacture when stored at 23°C +/-2°C and 50% +/-4% relative humidity

unmatched.reason: Shear Strength table values are in MPa and represent tensile/shear strength under multiple conditions, not explicit static shear holding time at 70C. No high-confidence existing dictionary key in snapshot fits this exactly.
Condition definitions should be retained as test-method context rather than business master data fields.
unmatched.source_label: Immediate State / Normal State / High Temperature / Heat-aging / Warm Water Immersion / Gasoline Immersion / Wax-remover immersion
details: Warm Water Immersion = 23°C, 24 hours -> 40°C water, 336 hours -> 23°C, 24 hours
Gasoline Immersion = 23°C, 24 hours -> Gasoline, 1 hour -> 23°C, 24 hours
Wax-remover immersion = 23°C, 24 hours -> Wax-remover, 1 hour -> 23°C, 24 hours
